seoTitle: How to Format a Book in Google Docs (2026 Full Guide)
metaDescription: Format a manuscript in Google Docs with proper trim size, chapter headings, section breaks, running headers, page numbers, and a live table of contents.
intro: Turning a raw manuscript into a print- or eBook-ready layout takes more than just typing chapters in order. This guide covers the core structural steps in Google Docs: setting your page trim size, defining heading styles for chapters, using section breaks to control page flow, and generating an automatic table of contents. Follow along to give your book a professional, publish-ready structure.

steps.heading: Set your book trim size and margins
steps.body: Open Page Setup and configure an industry-standard trim size, such as 6" x 9", with mirrored margins that suit print or eBook distribution.

steps.heading: Apply consistent heading styles
steps.body: Use Heading 1, 2, and 3 styles for your book title and chapter breaks so you get a clean typographic hierarchy throughout the manuscript.

steps.heading: Use section breaks to isolate chapters
steps.body: Insert Section Breaks (Next Page) rather than plain page breaks so each chapter's formatting, including headers and numbering, can be controlled independently.

steps.heading: Configure running headers and page numbers
steps.body: Turn on Different first page and unlink sections where needed so your chapter titles run cleanly in the header, and set page numbers to start on your first chapter page instead of the cover.

steps.heading: Generate a table of contents
steps.body: Once your headings are applied consistently, insert a dynamic table of contents that updates automatically with page numbers or dot leaders as you keep writing.

steps.heading: Export for print or eBook
steps.body: Run a final check on line spacing, indents, and layout consistency, then download your finished manuscript as a print-ready PDF from the File menu.

faq.question: Can Google Docs handle a full novel-length manuscript?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Docs can manage long manuscripts, though heavy use of section breaks and headings helps keep large documents organized and responsive.

faq.question: How do I stop page numbers from showing on my cover page?
faq.answer: Enable the Different first page option in your header and footer settings so your cover stays free of running numbers.

faq.question: Does the table of contents update automatically as I add chapters?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you use proper heading styles, the table of contents refreshes with a single click to reflect new pages and chapters.

faq.question: What file format should I export for self-publishing platforms?
faq.answer: A print-ready PDF exported from the File menu is generally the safest option for platforms like Kindle or IngramSpark.
